would be nice to start using it then to generate voice data
the audio token format i suggest atm is Moss-Audio Tokenizer V2
it is by far the best for music, sfx , speech
it is 400 tokens per sec, but can be decoded with  several tokens at one time with delay pattern decoding
https://necessary-velvet-generated-inns.trycloudflare.com/codec_comparison_2026.html
i think if you want to pretrain it at a reasonable scale you should only do a limited number of moss-audio
like a short sequence of something smaller, then followed by moss
so you get cross transfer
bc 400 tokens per sec would quickly overwhelme the dataset
that is if you want to do omni modal
if you just want to do audio, and don't care about tying it to language, it is fine to do just audio tokens - moss-audio
its a trade off

I think we humans do only remember last 10 seconds with all sensory details. This is the ultra short-term memory. It's actually less than 10 seconds. But 10 seconds is reasonable, so I would train systems that should understand and generate audio with all details for, let's say, 10 seconds, so 4,000 tokens. And everything that is longer away should be compressed with some specialized audio encoder into latents. Or maybe just provide the high-level caption and ASR of what can be heard further away. But this could be problematic for music. So, yeah, whatever is further away needs to be somehow compressed. And 10 or 20 seconds can be put into the full context. This problem does not go away with SNAC, it's just a little bit less extreme. But you get worse quality. So quality-wise, the MOSS audio tokenizer version 2 is the best you can get.
yeah - i agree the problem is there. we have a compression from speech/sound
it's called words
language words
and probably latent space ideas too

i think quality wise - it doesn't matter as much b/c we are pretraining. we can use any multiple audio codec from low to high. as long as we bind them to something common like language, or each other or to image tokens, or whatever
we can post-train on the higher end moss stuff
for 4K, maybe interleaved with words

i think we need a fast and efficient diffusion based model like you proposed
i think diffusion is like evolution - throughout random changes, the path to some semantically (functional) output is gotten
and that is how it can be so fast and efficient
but the jury is still out on whether we can get long term language controllable planning , etc. from diffusion, which we coudl arguably get from a LLM < 2b params
